rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. GRANT LOMBARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT GRANT LOMBARD's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Default and default judgment are set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473(d). Defendant has provided evidence that valid service of the summons and complaint was not effectuated because he did not live at the address where the substituted service occurred. (Declaration of Grant Lombard, Ex. A; County of San Diego v. Gorham (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1215, 1229, "once proof is made that the judgment is void based on extrinsic evidence, the judgment is said to be equally ineffective and unenforceable as if the judgment were void on its face because it violates constitutional due process.") The case is dismissed pursuant to CCP sec. 583.250. Defendant was not validly served with the summons and complaint within three years of the filing of the complaint. Plaintiff has argued no statutory basis that would allow for an extension of this mandatory time frame. (CCP sec. 583.250(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN HERSCHEL BORNSTEIN VS. ROUSSELL LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT ERIC ROUSSEL's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied. The motion is untimely. (CCP § 473(b).) Default judgment was entered on June 5, 2013. On July 7, 2013, Defendant Roussel filed a motion to set aside default/default judgment that was denied, without prejudice, for failure to provide a copy of the proof of service on August 20, 2013. On September 11, 2013, Defendant Roussel filed a motion to set aside/vacate judgment that was continued to December 2, 2013. On December 2, 2013, the motion was taken off calendar in light of the filing of an Amended Motion that was set for hearing on January 7, 2014. On January 7, 2014, the Court took the matter off calendar because no proof of service of motion was provided. On January 24, 2014, Defendant Roussel filed the instant motion, which was not timely filed within six months of default judgment. Defendant Roussel's reliance on CCP § 473.5 is not dispositive as Defendant Roussel admits service of summons that resulted in actual notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ZACKARY B. ANDERSON VS. DENNY'S et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Denny'S Corporation'S Motion For Terminating Sanction And/Or Other Sanctions, Including Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And/Or His Attorney In The Sum Of &3417.78
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT DENNY'S CORPORATION'S Motion For Terminating Sanction And/Or Other Sanctions, Including Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And/Or His Attorney In The Sum Of $3417.78. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to terminating sanctions. No opposition filed. Request for monetary sanctions: Denied in view of grant of terminating sanction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 19, DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY's Motion For Determinatuion Of Good Faith Settlement. Granted. rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P. and HILL-UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Adjudication. Granted. All requests for judicial notice are granted. The plain language of the Purchase And Sale Agreement, and the subsequent seven amendments signed by the parties, shows that the funds released from escrow to Defendant were in consideration for extension of the closing date. (Declaration of Nussbaum In Support Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Each Cause of Action Of the First Amended Complaint, Exhs. 1-8.) Plaintiffs' evidence does not raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether the funds were liquidated damages. (Declaration of Gao In Support of Opposition to UN Plaza's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, 27-31.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS HUFFMAN VS. EBAY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order As To Pltf'S Request For Document Production, Set Two, Request No. 7; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT EBAY, INC.'s Motion For Protective Order As To Plaintiff'S Request For Document Production, Set Two, Request No. 7; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part as follows: motion granted for protective order to designate materials as "confidential." Court finds that potential disclosure of trade secrets is adequate basis for issuance of protective order, and no countervailing public interest in disclosure has been demonstrated. Based on representation that the current further production of documents identified the necessity to seek an order now, rather than earlier, the Court does not find the request so untimely as to constitute a waiver. Request for sanctions by both parties is denied. (a) As to defendant's request, Court finds that the request for the protective order should have been made on or before the response to the original motion to compel further responses. However, the Court has discretion to order a protective order at this time under C.C.P. §2031.060 and so grants the motion. (b) As to plaintiff's request, the Court finds good cause to issue the protective order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WHITNEY R LEEMAN PH.D. VS. MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED's Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For A Protective Order. Denied. McCormick & Company shall produce its entity designees in San Francisco, the location notice by Plaintiff. Toyota Motor Corp. v. Superior Court is distinguishable from the instant action. The Toyota Court's decision was made in the context of individuals whose depositions had been noticed, not a corporation noticed for deposition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2025.230. The Toyota Court explicitly cautioned, "we express no opinion, however, as to whether our analysis or the conclusions we have reached in this opinion would or should extend or apply to a court order made pursuant to section 2025.230 which provides for the circumstance where the deponent named is not a natural person." (Toyota Motor Corp. v. Sup.Ct. (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1107, 1125, fn. 20 (internal quotations omitted).) Monetary sanctions of $ 3,500.00 are awarded in favor of Whitney R. Leeman and against McCormick & Company to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order . The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD CASTANEDA VS. WESTCO EQUITIES, INC et al***CASE TRANSFERRED TO FRESNO COUNTY***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT WESTCO EQUITIES, INC's Motion For Change Of Venue. Granted. (CCP § 395(a).) The Court finds that the contract was entered into in Fresno County and the contract was to be performed in Fresno County. Accordingly, transfer to Fresno County is proper. (CCP § 397(a).) The evidence proffered in reply is improper and was not considered by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP PAQUET VS. THE MINT B&R, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) Granted as to special interrogatory numbers 8, 9, 11, 13, 15, and 18. With respect to responses to nos. 9 and 13, the Court notes that the responses were vague either as to time or as to the purchaser. (2) Denied as to special interrogatory numbers 2, 3, 6, 10, 12, 14, and 16. The amended response contained sufficient responses to these interrogatories as propounded. Request for sanctions: granted in the amount of $250. No substantial justification exists to refuse to answer interrogatories on the grounds of relevancy. As to the balance of the granted interrogatories, substantial justification existed to merit the opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP PAQUET VS. THE MINT B&R, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE MINT B&R, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to form interrogatory numbers 2.3, 2.5, 6.5, 6.7, and 8.8. Court finds in form interrogatories that drivers license information may lead to some relevant evidence, particularly in view of the requested responses to form interrogatory no. 2.5. As to number 2.5, plaintiff should clarify residence address during the summer months of June and July while plaintiff was on summer vacation. Responses to numbers 6.5, 6.7 and 8.8 are also merited. Request for sanctions: Granted in the amount of $250. No substantial justification exists for relevancy objections or non-responses ("Discovery is ongoing" is not a sufficient response on its own). However, much of the information sought was supplied in other responses, and further responses are more likely to duplicate previously disclosed information rather than provide substantial new information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
